smartlead_campaign: OR #6 | Personalized Line | Forecast Lead Magnet | Without Other Contact Name

thread.subject: Braids and Wigs ad report
thread.body_text:
Maria, saw braids and wigs leans on same day shipping plus local delivery in about an hour, curious how that's been landing.

 separate note: I made Braids and Wigs a 4-min ad fatigue report from your live Meta ads. the one about to die, the duplicate eating budget, which to scale, and a fresh angle to test.

 most ecom brands in your niche usually run the same handful of ads too long. this spots it before ROAS drops.

 worth a look?

 Kyle H.
 Co-founder | OmniRocket

 P.S. if there's a better person for this, point me their way :)

thread.subject: RE: Braids and Wigs ad report
thread.body_text:
Maria - forgot to mention: the report also catches the ad Meta is quietly throttling. it's usually a near-copy of another ad that's eating budget without you noticing.

 Braids and Wigs's is still sitting ready on my end - want me to send it?

 - Kyle

 P.S. if it's not you, who should I reach out to?

thread.subject: RE: Braids and Wigs ad report
thread.body_text:
Maria - last check on this.

 should I shelve Braids and Wigs's fatigue report, or is there a better week to send it? no worries either way - just don't want to keep landing in your inbox.

 - Kyle

 P.S. if timing's just off, ping me in a few weeks - still happy to send.

Hi Kyle,
 

 Thank you for following up. Before we move forward, could you clarify what you'd need from us, specifically, did you need access to our Meta ad account to put this report together and if so, what level?
 

 Best regards,
 

 
 Maria Mushi 
 Manager – Braids and Wigs 
 London, Ontario 
 226-504-1321 
 www.braidsandwigs.ca

Hi Maria,

 great question, and the short answer is nothing needed on your end for what i already built.

here's the report btw: Braids and Wigs Ad Fatigue Report 

 this report was built entirely from public data. i pulled your live ad from meta's ad library and analyzed them from the outside, so no account access was involved at all.

 access only comes into play for the deeper work: a full audit plus a 30 and 90 day plan mapped to your actual numbers.

 the way we work is we hop on a quick call first, i get to understand your brand and where you're trying to go, and if we're a good fit we move forward. that's the point where i'd need access, and we'd start with read-only.

 worth a 30 min chat to see if it's a fit?

 Book a time here → Calendar 

 - Kyle

copy.tldr: Braids and Wigs is a Canadian value hair retailer running just one message-us ad with no reviews on site, so the play is to build 3 clear buyer angles and test them cheaply over 30 days, but first confirm they can actually take checkout orders and afford paid spend on a low average order value.

deep_dive.what_they_are: A Canadian online beauty store based in London, Ontario. They sell wigs, braiding hair, extensions, and hair and skin care from name brands like Sensationnel, Mane Concept, Outre, and Ampro. They run a big catalog, about 500 products.
deep_dive.what_they_sell: Wigs (human and synthetic), braiding and crochet hair, bundles, closures, plus hair care like gels, oils, shampoos, and makeup and accessories. Prices run from under a dollar for beads up to about $508 for premium human hair. Their median price is about $10.52, which we are using as a stand-in for average order value since we cannot see their real numbers.
deep_dive.positioning: High-variety value play. Name-brand looks at low prices, not a boutique premium house. They lean on deals, free shipping over $69.99, buy now pay later, and fast local delivery in London Ontario.
deep_dive.who_buys: Value-conscious Canadian women who wear protective styles like wigs, braids, weaves, and crochet. They want name-brand looks fast, affordably, and with pay-later options.
strengths: Strong offer stack already in place: free shipping over $69.99, free gift at $99.99, buy now pay later, and same-day local delivery in London.
Deep catalog of recognized brands, so there is a lot of creative and product angles to test.
gaps: Only one Meta ad seen running, and its call to action is Send message, not Shop now. That builds no checkout sales data and leans on manual chat.
No reviews anywhere on the site. There is no social proof to lean on in ads, which makes cold buyers slower to trust and harder to convert.

game_plan.headline: Turn one message-us ad into a real test of three buyer angles that drive shop-now sales, then find the one that wins.

phases.goal: Find which buyer angle and which audience actually drives sales at a price that works. This is a learning month, not a scaling month.
campaigns: One sales campaign with three ad sets, each built around one of the three avatars below. Same budget each so we can compare fairly.
One simple retargeting ad set for people who viewed products or added to cart but did not buy, since new buyers here need extra trust before they commit.
lead_products: Human Hair Kinky Headband Wig - Glueless & Easy Install ($123.34). Lead here for the 'quick and easy' angle. Headband wigs need no glue and install in seconds, which is the strongest hook for a first-time or busy buyer. The glueless easy-install line is a clear promise we can build a whole ad around.

Sensationnel Empire Human Hair Feather Crochet Braids - Ripple Wave 14 ($58.04). Lead here for the protective-style and braiding buyer. It is a known brand at a mid price, good for the value shopper who wants a name-brand look.

Mane Concept Brown Sugar Wig BS144 ($65.29, marked down from $101.57). Lead here for the deal-driven buyer. A big visible discount on a name brand is the exact hook this store's positioning is built on.
phases.creative: Simple, real-looking video and photo, not polished studio ads. Show the wig or braids going on and the finished look, using their own words like glueless, easy install, melted hairline, and without the huge price tag. One angle per avatar. Because there are no reviews yet, lean on the offer and the before/after look to build trust instead of star ratings. Point every ad to the product page with Shop now, not Send message.
phases.budget: Start around $50 CAD a day across the three ad sets, roughly $15 to $17 each. Hold it steady for the first 2 weeks so we can read results, then move budget to whichever angle is winning. Add a small retargeting budget of about $10 a day.
phases.what_success_looks_like: By day 30 we know which of the three angles gets the cheapest clicks and the most add-to-carts and sales, and we have at least one ad set producing sales at a cost that leaves margin. We are not trying to scale yet. We are trying to find the winner.

estimates.label: Starting budget
estimates.value: About $50 to $60 CAD a day to start
estimates.basis: Enough to get roughly 30 to 50 clicks a day at typical costs in this category, which is the floor for learning which of the three angles works within 30 days.

estimates.label: Revenue we would aim to add
estimates.value: We would need their real average order and margin before promising a revenue number
estimates.basis: Their median product price is about $10.52, which is too low to promise ad-driven revenue on its own. The higher-priced wigs (around $60 to $145) are what could make paid ads work, so we need to know their real average order size and profit per order first.

assumptions: The website checkout works and can take card orders, not just chat messages. If they mostly sell through DMs and local delivery, the plan changes.
The wigs priced $60 and up make up a real share of sales. If almost all orders are $10 accessories, paid ads will not carry the cost and we would need a bundle or minimum-order push instead.
They can supply or shoot simple phone video of products being worn. Without visuals, ads in this category struggle.

questions.question: Are you running any paid ads right now, and what are you spending a month?
questions.listening_for: We only saw one ad live. Find out if there is more we cannot see, and whether they have any budget habit at all. Zero spend means we start from scratch.

questions.question: Who handles the ads and the page today, you or someone else?
questions.listening_for: Whether Maria decides, or there is another person or a past agency. Also whether the Send message ad is being answered by hand, which does not scale.

questions.question: What is your typical order size, and do most orders include a wig or mostly the smaller items like gels and accessories?
questions.listening_for: This is the make-or-break number. If most orders are $10 accessories, ads are very hard. If wigs at $60 to $145 are common, we have room. We cannot see this, so ask directly.

questions.question: Roughly how much do you keep after product cost on a typical wig order?
questions.listening_for: Margin. If it is thin, we need higher order values or bundles to make paid ads pay. This decides how aggressive we can be.

questions.question: Last time you tried ads, what happened?
questions.listening_for: Whether they got burned before, what they tried, and what they blame. Sets expectations and tells us what not to repeat.

questions.question: Why does your current ad send people to message you instead of straight to buy on the site?
questions.listening_for: Is this a choice because chat converts better for them, or just how it was set up? If chat is doing the selling, that changes the whole funnel plan.

questions.question: When someone lands on a product page, do they usually buy, or drop off?
questions.listening_for: Where the leak is. If clicks are cheap but nobody buys, the problem is the page or trust, not the ad. The missing reviews likely hurt here.

questions.question: You have no reviews showing on the site. Do you collect them anywhere, like Instagram, texts, or in person?
questions.listening_for: Whether we can pull real customer proof into ads and the site. This is the biggest trust gap for cold buyers. Even screenshots help.

questions.question: What share of your sales is local London delivery versus shipping across Canada?
questions.listening_for: If most sales are local, ads targeting all of Canada will waste money. We may want to run local and national separately.

questions.question: Which products actually sell best for you right now?
questions.listening_for: Confirm or correct our guess. We picked the headband wig, the crochet braids, and the Brown Sugar deal wig. Their real bestseller should lead.

questions.question: Can you shoot simple phone video of wigs being put on and worn?
questions.listening_for: Whether we have creative to work with. In this category, seeing the install and the finished look is what sells. No visuals means slow going.

questions.question: Do you get many people finding you on Google when they search for wigs or braiding hair in Canada or in London?
questions.listening_for: People clearly search for these brand names and for 'braiding hair London Ontario', so there is a real Google and local search opening. Listen for whether they show up now or not at all.

questions.question: Are the brand names you carry, like Sensationnel and Mane Concept, ones people search for by name?
questions.listening_for: These are known brands people look up. That is honest search demand we could capture with Google, unlike a brand-new made-up product nobody searches for.

objections.objection: My products are cheap, so ads will never pay off.
objections.answer: You are right that a $10 order cannot carry ads on its own. That is why we would not advertise the gels or beads. We lead with the wigs at $60 to $145 and use your free shipping and free gift thresholds to push order size up. We confirm your real order size on this call before promising anything.

objections.objection: I do not have any reviews, will ads even work?
objections.answer: It makes cold buyers slower, but it is fixable. In the first 30 days we lean on the offer and on showing the wig actually worn, and we start pulling in any proof you have, even Instagram comments or texts. Building that proof is part of the work.

objections.objection: My current ad gets people messaging me and that works fine.
objections.answer: Chat can convert, but it does not scale and you cannot see which ad drives sales. We keep what works and add a shop-now path alongside it so you build real sales data and can grow without answering every message by hand.

objections.objection: Do you need to get into my ad account?
objections.answer: For the plan on this call, no. The report was built from public data. If we work together we start with read-only access so I can see what has worked before I change anything. You stay in full control.

red_flags.flag: Median product price is only about $10.52.
red_flags.why_it_matters: A lot of the catalog is too cheap to carry paid ads. Ads only work if the higher-priced wigs make up a real share of orders. We must confirm real order size before promising results.

red_flags.flag: No reviews anywhere on the site.
red_flags.why_it_matters: No social proof means cold buyers trust slower and convert worse. It makes ads harder to run and we have to build proof from scratch.

red_flags.flag: The only ad seen uses Send message, not Shop now.
red_flags.why_it_matters: This builds no checkout sales data and relies on someone answering messages by hand. We do not yet know if their site checkout is even the main way they sell.

red_flags.flag: The lead came from a gmail address (braidswigs@gmail.com) while the contact is maria@braidsandwigs.ca.
red_flags.why_it_matters: Small mismatch worth a light check that we are talking to the real decision maker and that the business is set up the way we assume.

other_channel_openings: Google Search: people search by name for the brands they carry, like Sensationnel and Mane Concept, and for terms like braiding hair and headband wigs. That is real existing demand we could capture.

Local SEO and Google for London Ontario: they offer same-day local delivery, so 'braiding hair London Ontario' and 'wig shop near me' type searches are a genuine local opening worth checking their current visibility on.
